factors.summary: Demand is accelerating due to strong production and inventory replenishment in several markets.

evidence.text: Freight revenues increased 14% year-over-year to $23.2 billion driven by higher fuel surcharge revenues, core pricing gains, and a 2% increase in volume. Volume increases were driven by strong production and inventory replenishment in the automotive industry, increased demand for coal due to higher natural gas prices, and continued strength in the industrial markets driven by rock, sand, and plastics.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand is accelerating due to strong production and inventory replenishment in several markets.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices, with core pricing gains contributing to revenue growth.

evidence.text: Freight revenues increased 14% year-over-year to $23.2 billion driven by higher fuel surcharge revenues, core pricing gains, and a 2% increase in volume.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is successfully raising prices, contributing to revenue growth.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is expected to accelerate in 2023 due to business development efforts.

evidence.text: We expect volume to outpace industrial production growth in 2023 due to our business development efforts bringing new customers to our railroad.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is expected to accelerate in 2023 due to business development efforts.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, particularly in automotive, coal, and industrial markets.

evidence.text: Volume increases were driven by strong production and inventory replenishment in the automotive industry, increased demand for coal due to higher natural gas prices, and continued strength in the industrial markets driven by rock, sand, and plastics.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are growing in automotive, coal, and industrial markets.

factors.summary: The fastest-growing end markets are automotive, industrial, and premium segments.

evidence.text: The automotive industry continued to recover from the shortage of semiconductors and the 2021 weather disruptions in the first quarter. Premium volume was flat compared to 2021 as the increased automotive shipments, domestic intermodal contract wins, and market strength due to tight truck capacity earlier in the year were more than offset by ongoing international supply chain disruptions and the soft market demand in the fourth quarter for domestic and parcel shipments.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed signals from market recovery and disruptions in premium segments.

factors.summary: Input costs, particularly fuel prices, are rising and impacting operational expenses.

evidence.text: The onset of the Russia-Ukraine conflict in late February 2022 drove crude oil prices above $100 a barrel, where it remained elevated until mid-2022, driving an increase in our average fuel price. While our average fuel price declined 8% in the fourth quarter from the second quarter high of $4.03, our average price in the fourth quarter was 46% higher than the fourth quarter of 2021.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Rising fuel prices are impacting operational expenses.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing due to labor agreements and wage inflation.

evidence.text: Higher fuel prices, operational challenges, inflation, increased volume-related costs, and a one-time charge for the labor agreements reached with our labor unions (See Labor Agreements in Other Matters in this Item 7 of Part II), drove a 20% increase in operating expenses.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are increasing due to agreements and wage inflation.

factors.summary: Operating margins are improving despite higher expenses, driven by revenue growth.

evidence.text: Increased revenues, due to higher fuel surcharge revenues, improved pricing, additional volume, and intermodal accessorial charges, more than offset the increased expenses producing operating income of $9.9 billion, a 6% increase over 2021.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Operating margins are improving despite higher expenses, driven by revenue growth.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving through initiatives to reduce variability and enhance operational performance.

evidence.text: We will continue to make capital investments targeted to improve operational performance, handle more volume, and increase efficiency, requiring fewer locomotives, freight cars, and other critical resources.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The summary and evidence indicate clear actions to enhance operational performance and efficiency.

factors.summary: Supply chain health is improving with steps to address congestion and enhance service reliability.

evidence.text: In 2023, we strive to increase reliability of our service product, reduce variability in network operations, and improve resource availability, including actively hiring additional train, engine, and yard employees.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Steps to address congestion and improve service reliability are supported by evidence of hiring and operational improvements.

factors.summary: Safety performance is improving with a decrease in reportable personal injury incidents.

evidence.text: As a result of these efforts, our reportable personal injury incidents rate per 200,000 employee-hours of 0.80 decreased 18% from 2021.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: A significant decrease in reportable personal injury incidents shows clear improvement in safety performance.

factors.summary: Management expects continued improvement in financial and operating results in 2023.

evidence.text: We expect volume to outpace industrial production growth in 2023 due to our business development efforts bringing new customers to our railroad. In the current environment, we expect continued operating ratio improvement driven by pricing in excess of inflation, improving our service product, and better leveraging our resources.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management expects continued improvement in financial and operating results, supported by specific business developments.

factors.summary: Management is confident with their strategy, as evidenced by share repurchases and capital investments.

evidence.text: We expect to generate strong cash flow from operating activities allowing us to continue our industry leading dividend payout ratio and commit excess cash to our share repurchase programs.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Confidence is evident through strong cash flow expectations and investments in share repurchase programs.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include macroeconomic uncertainties and volatile fuel prices.

evidence.text: Macroeconomic uncertainties remain in 2023 that could have a material impact on our 2023 financial and operating results. Significant changes in fuel prices could have an impact on consumer discretionary spending, impacting demand for various consumer products we transport.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Macroeconomic uncertainties and volatile fuel prices pose clear risks to near-term financial results.

factors.summary: Fuel prices and market conditions are key sensitivities impacting near-term results.

evidence.text: Projections for crude oil and natural gas continue to fluctuate in the current economic environment. We could again see volatile fuel prices during 2023, as they are sensitive to global and U.S. domestic demand, refining capacity, geopolitical events, weather conditions, and other factors.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: While fuel prices are a key sensitivity, the evidence doesn't indicate a clear positive or negative impact yet.

factors.summary: The company is in growth mode, hiring additional employees to address network congestion.

evidence.text: We aggressively hired and graduated 1,302 new train, engine, and yard employees; temporarily relocated train, engine, and yard employees to areas with the greatest need; added locomotives to the fleet in select locations; and reduced freight car inventory, relative to carloads, from our network.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Hiring additional employees to address network congestion shows growth and operational improvement.

factors.summary: Labor relations are stable with no active or potential strike risks mentioned.

evidence.text: Safety procedures and policies are refined based on Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) guidelines.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Stable labor relations with refined safety procedures indicate a positive environment.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are increasing in 2023 to support growth and efficiency.

evidence.text: In 2023, we expect our capital plan to be approximately $3.6 billion, up 6% from 2022 as we make investments to support our growth strategy.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increasing capital expenditures support growth and efficiency, indicating a positive outlook.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are maintained through dividend payouts and share repurchases.

evidence.text: We expect to generate strong cash flow from operating activities allowing us to continue our industry leading dividend payout ratio and commit excess cash to our share repurchase programs.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Maintained shareholder returns through dividends and repurchases, supported by strong cash flow.

factors.summary: Free cash flow is improving, supporting shareholder returns and investments.

evidence.text: Free cash flow is defined as cash provided by operating activities less cash used in investing activities and dividends paid. Free cash flow is not considered a financial measure under GAAP by SEC Regulation G and Item 10 of SEC Regulation S-K and may not be defined and calculated by other companies in the same manner.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improving free cash flow supports both shareholder returns and investments, indicating a positive trend.
